text: I once stayed silent during a bullying incident and felt ashamed afterward.
A coach ignored my pain during training, and I learned to say stop earlier.
When I clearly explained my limits at work, the conflict de-escalated.
I get tense when instructions are repeated in a commanding tone.
I once froze under stress and wished I had voiced discomfort sooner.
After a difficult incident, being heard and acknowledged reduced my panic.
I distrust situations where harm is framed as 'just procedure'.
